title: OpenAI Hardware Biz Built with Apple Secrets, Apple Says
summary: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Al Alleged Trade Secret TheftApple accused OpenAI of building its secretive new device with stolen trade secrets in a lawsuit demanding monetary damages and the return of stolen intellectual property. OpenAI’s nascent hardware is "rotten to its core by its illegal reliance on misappropriated trade secrets," Apple alleged.

title: When Hackers Cut the Internet, Will the Water Still Flow?
summary: EPA Drills Water Utilities to DisconnectIt's 4:45 a.m. and the internet has gone down. For the next three days, a fictional water utility will have to figure out how to keep its 120,000 customers' water supply flowing and safe, without the ubiquitous connectivity they, and the rest of us, take for granted.

title: Infoblox to Acquire Kentik for Unified Network Visibility
summary: SaaS Observability Vendor Adds Real-Time Network Flow Telemetry to DNS IntelligenceInfoblox plans to acquire San Francisco-based network observability specialist Kentik to combine DNS, DHCP, asset intelligence and real-time network flow telemetry into a unified platform designed to help enterprises manage increasingly complex hybrid cloud and AI-driven environments.

title: FDIC Issues Guidance to Areas in Illinois Impacted by Severe Storms
summary: The FDIC has announced a series of steps intended to provide regulatory relief to financial institutions and facilitate recovery in areas of Illinois affected by severe storms, straight-line winds, and tornadoes.

title: FDIC: Institutions Encouraged to Work with Borrowers Impacted by Shutdown
summary: Five federal regulatory agencies encourage financial institutions to work with customers affected by the federal government shutdown.

title: FFIEC: Statement on End of Microsoft Support for Windows XP
summary: The FFIEC on Oct. 7 issued a joint statement concerning Microsoft's discontinuation of support for its Windows XP operating system as of April 8, 2014.

title: FDIC: Supervisory Approach to Payment Processing Relationships with Merchant Customers
summary: The FDIC is clarifying its policy and supervisory approach related to facilitating payment processing services directly, or indirectly through a third party, for merchant customers engaged in higher-risk activities.

title: DoD: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking on Privacy Training
summary: The Department of Defense and two other government agencies have issued a proposed rule designed to help ensure that government contractors provide adequate privacy training to their staff members.

title: Annual Report to Congress on Breaches of Unsecured Protected Health Information
summary: The Department of Health and Human Services' Office for Civil Rights provided a report to Congress on health information breaches from September 2009 through 2010, as required under the HITECH Act. Nearly 7.9 million Americans were affected by almost 30,800 health information breaches, according to the report.

title: FFIEC Final Authentication Guidance
summary: The Final FFIEC Guidance has been issued and its main intent is to reinforce the 2005 Guidance's risk management framework and update the Agencies' expectations regarding customer authentication, layered security, or other controls in the increasingly hostile online environment.

title: Accounting of Disclosures Under the HITECH Act
summary: A notice of proposed rulemaking from the HHS Office for Civil Rights that would modify the HIPAA Privacy Rule standard for accounting of disclosures of protected health information and add new requirements for access reports.

title: ENISA: Software vulnerability prevention initiatives
summary: The European Network and Information Security Agency, ENISA, has compiled a list of existing initiatives focused on finding and preventing software vulnerabilities.

title: Building Our Future Together
summary: In my first weeks as Executive Director of EFF, I’ve been reminded every day how consequential this moment is in determining what kind of future we will have. We are on the edge. What each one of us steps up to do – with our expertise, energy, and resources – will determine whether our future is one of openness, security, and fundamental rights, or one controlled through fear, surveillance, and centralized power. summary: Zimbra is urging customers to apply updates to address a critical security vulnerability impacting the Classic Web Client that could result in arbitrary code execution. title: Injective Labs GitHub Compromise Pushes Wallet-Key-Stealing npm Packages
summary: Unknown threat actors compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and leveraged it to publish a malicious package on the npm registry to steal cryptocurrency wallet private keys and mnemonic seed phrases. title: Six New U-Boot Flaws Could Let Malicious Images Crash Devices or Run Code at Boot
summary: Researchers at firmware security firm Binarly have found six new flaws in U-Boot, the small program that starts up hardware as varied as home routers, smart cameras, and the management chips inside data-center servers. title: Laser Attack Resets Tangem Wallet Passwords on Cards That Can't Be Patched
summary: Researchers at Ledger's Donjon security team have shown that a precisely timed laser pulse, aimed at the chip inside a Tangem crypto wallet card, can reset the card's password to anything the attacker picks. title: Unpatched XRING Flaw in XQUIC Lets Remote Clients Crash HTTP/3 Servers
summary: A single wrong variable on one line in XQUIC, Alibaba's QUIC and HTTP/3 library, lets any remote client crash the server with a short burst of completely legal traffic. There is no patch. title: Hypothesis Testing Is Just a Courtroom With Worse Snacks
summary: Hypothesis Testing Explained for BeginnersNobody faked the data. One little number did all the lying, and it’s the same number deciding what you read, buy, and swallowIn the spring of 2015, newspapers across twenty countries told their readers that chocolate helps you lose weight.This wasn’t a tabloid rumor. There was a real study behind it. Real volunteers ate real chocolate, real scientists took real measurements, and the result was statistically significant, which is science’s way of stamping a finding as legit. title: GPT-5.6: What Actually Changed on Your Bill
summary: Read the diff, not the demo. Field notes on the GPT-5.5 to 5.6 migration, where the flagship price held but a few things that used to be free now cost money and several defaults moved underfoot.The short versionThe flagship price did not move. gpt-5.6-sol matches 5.5 at five dollars in and thirty out per million tokens. The changes that move your bill are underneath the headline.Cache writes, free on 5.5, now cost 1.25 times the input rate on 5.6. Caching stopped being risk-free and became a bet that pays off from the second reuse.Your cache-read percentage cannot tell you the exposure. Writes

title: Paper Walkthrough — Geometrically-Constrained Agent for Spatial Reasoning
summary: How a formal task constraint bridges the semantic-to-geometric gap in spatial reasoning VLMsCan a vision-language model (VLM) imagine sitting on a sofa and see where the coffee is?Logically, it’s possible — work out the sofa’s orientation, rotate its imagined viewpoint accordingly, and locate the coffee table relative to that viewpoint — but it falls apart in reality. The classical ReAct loop has no built-in mechanism to fix that ambiguity before acting on it.This is not a knowledge problem. title: Tricks for Using AI Well
summary: Empirically Backed Patterns and Myths the Research Has DebunkedOperational flow of the Chain-of-Verification (CoVe) method. A structured four-step prompting strategy to mitigate hallucinations in LLMs by generating control questions and cross-verifying them in isolation.The model discovers inconsistencies that it would overlook in the integrated version. The separation between generating and verifying is what makes it work: if you ask for everything at once in a single prompt, the model “looks at” its own response and confirms what it already said, perpetuating the same error. title: Building an Agent Is Cheap Now. Proving It Works Is the Skill.
summary: Part four of the PromptProof series. Turning an agent’s recorded runs into a grade you can defend, with four dimensions of quality, an LLM judge held under courtroom rules, and a CI gate that turns red when behaviour slips.This is Part four of a build-in-public series, and a quick map helps if you are joining now. Part one and two built PromptProof, an engine that makes a single prompt prove itself, with typed gates, retries that carry their own error message, and a feedback loop that revises an output until it passes. title: Craft still matters, but it’s about outcomes
summary: The craft never lived in the metal. When the medium changed, judgment moved to a new address.AI moved craft from production to judgment; the same tools can help you protect it, if you describe your standards and keep humans reviewing.For a decade, design proved itself in the artifact: A pixel-perfect Figma file, a prototype that clicked exactly right, a design system with everything perfectly documented.That proved you were good.A Figma frame has never shipped revenue — It promises a product but it is not one, and we spent years mistaking the two.Design was never supposed to be about productio
